prompt: Rewrite this technical architecture doc so the VP of Sales can understand it in a 5-minute read.
Take this formal incident report and turn it into a casual Slack update for the engineering channel.
I have API documentation written for backend developers. Rewrite it for frontend devs who just need to know the endpoints and payloads.
This research paper summary is too academic. Repackage it as a blog post for a general tech audience without dumbing down the conclusions.
Convert this developer changelog into user-facing release notes. Same features, different voice.
Our internal security policy is written in legalese. Rewrite it so new hires actually understand what they're supposed to do, but keep every requirement intact.
Take this CLI tool's man page and rewrite it as a friendly getting-started guide for non-technical users.
I need this investor update rewritten for the board. Same numbers, same story, but more formal tone and executive structure.
Adapt this English-language README so it reads naturally for a Japanese developer audience — adjust idioms and cultural references but keep the technical content identical.
This postmortem is written for SREs. Rewrite it for product managers — they need to understand what happened and what it means for the roadmap, not the kernel traces.
